module.title: Week 31: Web Application Attacks
module.output: Web app vulnerability report from testing

topics.title: Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview
topics.description: Understand the most critical web application risks
topics.content:
## Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview

> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand the most critical web application risks
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain OWASP Top 10 with real-world examples for each category.
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the most common OWASP category?
topics.interviewAnswer: A01 Broken Access Control is the most common, present in 94% of tested applications. It occurs when users can access functionality or data they shouldn't - like viewing another user's account or accessing admin pages without authorization.

quiz.question: A01 Broken Access Control involves?
A03 Injection includes?
A10 SSRF stands for?
2021 OWASP categories count?
DVWA is used for?
Which best describes Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: OWASP Top 10 Overview can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: SQL Injection
topics.description: Exploit and mitigate SQL injection vulnerabilities
topics.content:
## Day 2: SQL Injection - Lab Exercise

> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: SQL Injection is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Exploit
- Mitigate SQL injection vulnerabilities
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Manual and automated SQL injection exploitation with data extraction.
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the difference between UNION-based and blind SQL injection?
topics.interviewAnswer: UNION-based SQLi uses UNION SELECT to add results directly to the query output - you see the data in the response. Blind SQLi is used when no data is returned to the screen - infer information from true/false responses (boolean blind) or time delays (time-based blind).

quiz.question: SQLi impact?
UNION-based SQLi requires?
Blind SQLi uses?
sqlmap automation tool?
Best SQLi mitigation?
Which best describes Day 2: SQL Injection?

topics.title: Day 3: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
topics.content:
## Day 3: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) - Lab Exercise

> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Exploit
- Prevent XSS vulnerabilities
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Exploit reflected, stored, and DOM-based XSS with real-world payloads.
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the difference between reflected, stored, and DOM-based XSS?
topics.interviewAnswer: Reflected: payload in URL/request, executes once when visited. Stored: payload saved on server (comment, profile), executes for every visitor. DOM-based: payload exploits client-side JS that modifies DOM, never reaches server. Stored is most dangerous (affects all users).

quiz.question: Reflected XSS executes?
Stored XSS affects?
XSS can steal?
CSP blocks?
HttpOnly cookie prevents?
Which best describes Day 3: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)?
quiz.explanation: Day 3: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 4: CSRF, SSRF, and Other Attacks
topics.description: Understand CSRF, SSRF, and file inclusion vulnerabilities
topics.content:
## Day 4: CSRF, SSRF, and Other Attacks

> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: CSRF, SSRF, and Other Attacks is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand CSRF
- SSRF
- File inclusion vulnerabilities
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Exploit CSRF, SSRF, LFI, and XXE vulnerabilities with practical examples.
topics.interviewQuestion: Why is SSRF particularly dangerous in cloud environments?
topics.interviewAnswer: SSRF can access cloud metadata services (AWS: http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/) which returns IAM credentials. With IMDSv1, SSRF directly steals cloud credentials. IMDSv2 adds a session token requirement to mitigate this. SSRF can also scan internal networks behind NAT.

quiz.question: CSRF exploits?

LFI reads?

AWS metadata URL?

topics.title: Day 5: Web App Pentest Capstone
topics.content:
## Day 5: Web App Pentest Capstone - Project

> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Web App Pentest Capstone is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Complete web application penetration test
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Walk through a complete web application penetration test methodology.
topics.interviewQuestion: What distinguishes a professional web app pentest from running automated scanners?
topics.interviewAnswer: Manual testing finds business logic flaws that scanners miss. Examples: privilege escalation by changing user IDs in requests, workflow bypass (adding items after checkout), parameter tampering (changing price), and race conditions. Professional pentesters understand the application's purpose and test for abuse cases.

quiz.question: Scanners miss?
Privilege escalation test?
Business logic tests?
API testing checks?
Professional pentest includes?
Which best describes Day 5: Web App Pentest Capstone?
